protocol.gt_removed: proof bodies replaced with admit() before agent launch

variants.B3: B1 + proven lemma registry (exact names + signatures)

key_finding_1.title: Proven lemma registry is the #1 context supplement
key_finding_1.detail: B3 (registry) goes from 2/6 (B1) to 4/6, including F5_hard which failed ALL 18a variants (0/4). The registry provides exact lemma names, preventing hallucination.
key_finding_1.evidence: F5_hard B3 PASSES at 91 LOC — the 18a universal failure was purely a lemma name problem, not a reasoning problem.

key_finding_2.title: Strategy hints alone don't help medium/hard functions
key_finding_2.detail: B2 (strategy) only helps F2_easy (where it provides the p_gt_2() reminder). For medium+ functions, knowing WHAT to do (strategy) doesn't help if you don't know the exact lemma NAMES (registry).
key_finding_2.evidence: B2 fails F3, F4, F5 — same as B1. Strategy without tools is empty advice.

key_finding_3.title: Full context (B4) ≈ registry (B3) with different coverage
key_finding_3.detail: B4 passes 4/6 like B3, but on different functions. B4 gets F2 (via strategy) but loses F5 (too much context → longer/worse proof at 170 LOC). B3 gets F5 (focused registry) but loses F2 (no strategy hint for p_gt_2).
key_finding_3.evidence: Union of B3+B4 would be 5/6. Adding spec definitions may actually hurt by bloating context.

key_finding_4.title: F5_hard solved by registry alone
key_finding_4.detail: The 18a universal failure (0/4 prompt variants) was broken by B3 which provides exact lemma names. The proof at 91 LOC is correct Verus. This confirms the 18a diagnosis: the model's REASONING was correct but NAMES were wrong.
key_finding_4.note: B4 (which includes registry + more) fails F5 at 170 LOC — too much context caused a bloated, incorrect proof. Registry alone is better.

key_finding_5.title: B1 baseline is weaker than 18a V1
key_finding_5.detail: B1 only gets 2/6 vs 18a V1's 4/6. This is because 18b's B1 prompt uses a simpler structure (no full mechanical lemma list from 18a). The 18a prompts included 60+ mechanically-extracted lemma signatures which served as a partial registry.
key_finding_5.note: This means the 18a 'available lemmas' section was partially doing what B3 does explicitly.

comparison_to_18a.combined_best: 6/6 would require B3 for F5 + any 18a variant for F6 (B3 loses F6)

always_include: proven lemma registry with exact signatures (18b finding)
include_when_available: strategy hint (helps easy functions, provides boilerplate reminders)
avoid: spec definitions in prompt (bloats context, may hurt hard functions)
pipeline_recommendation.optimal_prompt: B3 (sibling + registry) as default, add strategy hint for known gotchas (p_gt_2, by(bit_vector) etc.)
